page: Amazon Web Services c7gn.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the 64-bit ARMv8 architecture. It features an AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.6 GHz, with no simultaneous multithreading. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage.

This instance is optimized for high-bandwidth network and EBS operations, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the c7gn.4xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, with its write large block memory bandwidth of 96.92 GB/sec and PassMark memory score of 2953.84 ranking in the top 25 percent of tested hardware. Most general CPU benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, fall within the average range.

A notable performance tradeoff is observed in cryptographic operations, where the PassMark CPU encryption test scores in the bottom 25 percent. Similarly, LLM inference for medium 7B models shows weak prompt processing speeds of 29.53 tokens/sec. The instance is suited for network-intensive compute workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and database operations that benefit from dedicated ARM cores and high network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by an AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance is designed for high-throughput networking, providing a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates strong memory write performance and memory scores, placing in the top 25 percent, while general compute and database operations perform in the average range. However, cryptographic performance is a notable tradeoff, scoring in the bottom 25 percent on encryption tests. This instance is suited for network-intensive compute tasks, static web serving, software compilation, and distributed database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for network-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.4xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 32 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores, high-speed memory write bandwidth, and 50 Gbps baseline network performance.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 cores at 2.6 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Delivers a high baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps
Strong memory write bandwidth and memory benchmark scores
Lower relative performance in cryptographic encryption and large LLM tasks
Suited for network-intensive compute, web serving, and software compilation
